title: Unified performance test for viscoelastic materials
abstract: The use of recycled materials can have significant economic value. With the increasing quantity of recycled material used in viscoelastic materials, especially asphalt mixture, understanding how they interact with original materials to produce a mixture that performs successfully, becomes critical. Currently, the technology to determine the effect of additives on the performance of asphalt mixture is lacking. The present invention relates to a new unified methodology for mechanical testing of asphalt mixture and other viscoelastic materials that improves the current practice in speed, convenience, and accuracy. A new improved specimen mounting method on Dynamic Shear Rheometer (DSR), a new recovery method for fine portion of asphalt mixture, and three new tests for the performance of recovered material using DSR is disclosed. The new methods provide performance grading of asphalt mixtures that is new to the industry and provide necessary tools for determining the effect of recycled materials on performance.
claims:
Claims A unified test method of mechanical properties of viscoelastic materials (VEM) using a Dynamic Shear Rheometer (DSR) having a first and a second parallel plate configuration and plate diameter of less than 25-mm comprising: Placing the VEM between the first and second plates; setting the temperature to a predetermined high-temperature above 30Â°C and applying one or more Pulse Loads Series (PLS) sufficient to cause appreciable amount of deformation in the VEM, wherein the VEM reaches a Stable Region wherein DA is a negative number approaches zero and does not reach an unstable region wherein DA becomes positive, and or setting the temperature to a predetermined low-temperature equal or less than 0Â°C and applying one or more Constant Load Series (CLS) sufficient to cause appreciable amount of deformation in the VEM, wherein the VEM reaches Stable Region and does not reach an Unstable Region, and or setting an intermediate temperature between 0 and 40Â°C and applying a Pulse Load Series sufficient to cause appreciable amount of deformation in the sample, wherein the VEM reaches Stable Region and does not reach Unstable Region, and further applying different iterations with the same Pulse Load Series at higher temperatures until the test reaches Unstable Region. The method of claim 1, wherein the VEM is one or more of the oil, powder, melts, paint, polymer, gum, polymer modified, rubber, rubber products, original asphalt binder, aged asphalt binder, extracted asphalt binder from mixture, and or Asphalt Mixture Residue. The method of claim 1, wherein the each of the first and second plates have a diameter that is 8 mm, and the gap between the parallel first and second plates is 0.5 mm for asphalt binder and 1.0 mm for Asphalt Mixture Residue. The method of claim 3, wherein the temperature at a predetermined deformation per cycle at Stable Region (SDC) for asphalt binder is the binder Performance Grade (PG) for high and low temperature test. The method of claim 3, wherein the temperature at a predetermined deformation per cycle at Stable Region (SDC) for Asphalt Minder Residue (AMR) is the Asphalt Mixture Index (AMI) for high and low temperature test. The method of claim 5, wherein the AMI is the grade of asphalt mixture at low, high and normal temperatures and determines the effect of recycled material on the original binder. 7. A method of mounting a VEM on a gap between a first plate and a second plate of the parallel plate rheometer comprising: a. weighting an appropriate amount of the VEM to fill the gap of a predetermined thickness and create a bulge, 

 b. placing the VEM on the first plate surface, c. spreading the VEM over the first surface in order to create material flow over the surface, d. heating the VEM to a first temperature until it melts and substantially covers the entire plate surface creating a symmetrical dome-shaped appearance, e. reducing the temperature to a second temperature suitable for adhesion of the VEM to the second plate; f. moving the first plate and the second plate towards each other to the predetermined gap, and g. allowing sufficient time for the VEM to adhere to the second plate. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the VEM is one or more of the oil, powder, melts, paint, polymer, polymer modified, rubber, rubber products, original asphalt binder, aged asphalt binder, extracted asphalt binder from mixture, and or Asphalt Mixture Residue. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the DSR plate diameter is 8 mm, and the gap between parallel plates is 0.5 mm for asphalt binder and 1.0 mm for Asphalt Mixture Residue. 10. The method of claim 7, wherein the DSR plate diameter is 25 mm, and the gap between parallel plates is 1.0 mm for asphalt binder. 11. The method of recovering Asphalt Mixture Residue (AMR) from asphalt mixture, comprising; a. Heating the asphalt mixture to a mixing temperature until it becomes soft, b. Evenly spreading the heated asphalt mixture over a non-absorbing surface, c. Continue heating and Periodically mixing and spreading the asphalt mixture, d. Repeat mixing and spreading the mixture while heated until a fine residue is attached on the non-absorbing surface, e. Discarding the mixture from the non-absorbing surface, f. Cooling the non-absorbing surface to room temperature, g. Shaving off the sands and stones from the non-absorbing surface until only fine portion of asphalt material remains on the surface, h. Collecting AMR. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the fine portion of asphalt mixture is substantially less than 0.25 mm in diameter. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the fine portion of asphalt mixture is substantially less than 0.33 mm in diameter. 14. The method of claim 11, wherein the fine portion of asphalt mixture is substantially less than 0.50 mm in diameter. 15. The method of claim 11, wherein the fine portion of asphalt mixture is substantially less than 1.0 mm in diameter. 16. The method of claim 11, wherein the non-absorbing surface is flat, or curved. 17. The method of claim 11, wherein the mixing is either of manual or mechanical.
